generic · model-selected fallback classification confidence 0.45 3 open unknowns status: refined · scope on hold

Untangle what each runtime directory is actually for

Pin down — and write down somewhere durable — what each runtime directory the system uses is actually for, because the boundaries between .cast/, build/, and goals/ keep surprising me mid-task and I lose time rediscovering them.

What this is
A captured, recurring friction — not yet a defined deliverable.
Outcome wanted
The directory boundaries confirmed against the code and durably recorded.
Final shape
Genuinely open — doc, refactor, or a note. Decided later.
What this is about

A real friction, captured before it dissolves

This is something that keeps costing time: rediscovering what each runtime directory is for. It is being written down now, before it quietly turns into "I'll just remember next time" — which won't happen. The job is to pin the boundaries down for real; its final form is deliberately left open.

Investigation

Trace where render jobs, tracking symlinks, and goal artifacts each actually land — and confirm it against the code, not against memory.

Cleanup

If two areas overlap, or a path is computed in more than one place, straighten it out.

Documentation

Even if nothing changes, one durable paragraph that names each directory's job would save the next confused moment.

The work spans all three modes and settles into none — which is exactly why it doesn't fit a tidy family. That ambiguity is part of the point, not a flaw to be papered over.

Working hunch — to confirm, not yet established. The current mental model the investigation needs to verify against the code:
.cast/ per-goal runtime tracking links
build/ non-goal, non-CI scratch
goals/<slug>/ canonical artifacts
Open questions

What still needs deciding

The shape of the outcome is unsettled, and that is the honest current state — not an omission. These three threads stay open; none is resolved or ranked here.

  • Open
    Is the right deliverable a short reference doc, a refactor that removes an overlap, or both — and does that decision change which family this work even is?
  • Open
    Are there more than three runtime areas in play — does the alembic/migration scratch or the test-fixtures directory count — or are those clearly out of this scope?
  • Open
    Who else trips over this? Is it just me, or is it worth a shared note in the repo rather than a personal one?

Classified generic (confidence 0.45; nearest alternative data_analysis) — a cross-cutting note that mixes investigation, cleanup, and documentation without any single family dominating. This is the unmatched-fallback representative by construction.
